recurring_innovation_gaps.name: SSRF Bypass Prevention After Browser Interactions
recurring_innovation_gaps.reason: Now enforced on snapshot/screenshot/tab routes in OpenClaw. Has appeared in multiple consecutive releases with escalating scope. We should build SSRF protection for our browser automation tools.

recurring_innovation_gaps.name: Gateway Sessions: Clear Auto-Fallback Model Overrides
recurring_innovation_gaps.reason: Session routing metadata isolation fix covers the routing metadata angle. Model override clearing is a separate concern.

recurring_innovation_gaps.name: Dotenv Security: Block Runtime-Control Env Vars
recurring_innovation_gaps.reason: No direct match in this release. Keep monitoring.

features.name: Force owner downgrade for untrusted hook:wake events
features.gap_analysis: Our heartbeat service dispatches hook events but does not enforce owner-role downgrade for system-triggered wake events. A malicious or misconfigured external trigger could gain elevated agent context.
features.implementation_notes: Add source_trust_level field to GatewayRequest. Force lowest-trust role for webhook/external triggers before dispatching to agent turn. Check trust level in gateway_server.py before elevating session capabilities.

features.name: Enforce SSRF policy on snapshot, screenshot, and tab routes
features.gap_analysis: Our browser automation via agent-browser MCP does not enforce SSRF policy on URLs passed to snapshot/screenshot/tab routes. An agent could request snapshots of internal services.
features.implementation_notes: Add SSRF validation middleware for browser tools accepting URL parameters. Block private IP ranges (10.x, 172.16-31.x, 192.168.x, 127.x, 169.254.169.254, ::1). Add URL resolution + IP range check before passing to Playwright/CDP. Include loopback bypass for own CDP control plane.

features.name: Redact sourceConfig and runtimeConfig alias fields
features.gap_analysis: Our gateway likely exposes configuration in debug/diagnostic endpoints. If alias fields contain secrets, they may leak through config snapshot APIs.
features.implementation_notes: Audit all config snapshot/health-check endpoints. Add redact_config() function that strips/masks known secret patterns and REDACTED_CONFIG_KEYS set. Apply to all /api/config/* and debug endpoints.

features.name: Prevent empty approver list from granting authorization
features.gap_analysis: If our approval/permission system has a default-deny posture, an empty approver list might accidentally grant access due to deny-by-default vs deny-by-empty-list ambiguity.
features.implementation_notes: Verify permission evaluation logic in guardrails/tool_schema.py. Ensure empty/null approver list evaluates as DENY. Add explicit test: permissions=[] -> action blocked.

features.name: Broaden shell-wrapper detection, block env-argv injection
features.gap_analysis: Our execution engine runs shell commands. If an agent crafts 'env VAR=value /bin/bash -c ...', it could bypass shell wrapper restrictions.
features.implementation_notes: Add detection for env-argv assignment patterns in execution engine shell command sanitization. Catch 'env' as command prefix and validate full argv chain.

features.name: Remove busybox/toybox from safe bins
features.gap_analysis: Sandbox binary allowlist change specific to OpenClaw's container security. We don't have an equivalent mechanism.

features.name: Gateway session routing metadata isolation (heartbeat/cron poisoning)
features.gap_analysis: Our heartbeat service creates sessions via InProcessGateway and sets routing metadata. If a heartbeat turn modifies shared session state, subsequent cron/user turns could be poisoned with wrong routing target.
features.implementation_notes: Ensure synthetic turns (heartbeat, cron) use isolated GatewaySession objects or reset routing metadata after each turn. Add session_context_isolation flag. Clean routing metadata at turn completion. Review how GatewaySession state persists across turns.

features.name: Gateway entrypoint resolution unification
features.gap_analysis: JS entrypoint path drift in OpenClaw's Node.js gateway. Our Python gateway doesn't have this issue.

features.name: Add commands.list RPC for command discovery
features.gap_analysis: We have a rich skill system but no unified RPC endpoint to discover available commands/skills at runtime. External clients must hardcode knowledge of available capabilities.
features.implementation_notes: Add /api/commands endpoint returning available skills, slash commands, and registered tool capabilities with metadata. Study OpenClaw's commands.list RPC schema for response format.

features.name: Auth hardening: blank example credentials, startup fail on placeholder
features.gap_analysis: Our .env.example or example config files could contain placeholder credentials that get accidentally deployed. Infisical mitigates this for managed secrets, but bootstrap configs may not.
features.implementation_notes: Audit all .env.example and config.example.* files. Ensure placeholders are clearly marked (YOUR_TOKEN_HERE). Add startup validation refusing to start if placeholder pattern detected in live config.

features.name: WebSocket keepalive tick broadcast fix
features.gap_analysis: Our WebSocket implementation may not have this backpressure issue. Low priority unless we observe tick-timeout disconnects.
features.implementation_notes: Monitor for WebSocket disconnects during long tasks. If observed, ensure keepalive frames are not droppable.

features.name: Stop cron refire loops on invalid next-run
features.gap_analysis: Our cron service uses croniter. If cron expression becomes invalid or next-run returns no valid future slot, scheduler could enter tight retry loop.
features.implementation_notes: Add guard: if croniter.get_next() raises or returns past time, do NOT immediately retry. Set job to errored state with backoff, schedule maintenance check. Add max retry rate limiter.

features.name: Preserve error-backoff floor during maintenance repair
features.gap_analysis: If cron service has error-backoff mechanism, maintenance repair recomputing next-run should NOT reset backoff to zero. Transiently-failing job could fire immediately after restart.
features.implementation_notes: Persist error-backoff floor in database alongside job state. On maintenance repair: use max(computed_next_run, current_time + error_backoff_floor). Only reset backoff on explicit user action.

features.name: Maintenance wake for unscheduled jobs
features.gap_analysis: OpenClaw keeps maintenance wake armed so enabled-but-unscheduled jobs recover when schedule is fixed. Our cron service lacks this self-healing.
features.implementation_notes: Add periodic maintenance sweep (every 5-10 min) that re-evaluates error/unschedulable jobs. If cron expression now valid, re-enable. Prevents manual restart after fixing broken expression.

features.name: Active Memory plugin (proactive recall sub-agent)
features.gap_analysis: Most architecturally significant feature. OpenClaw runs dedicated memory sub-agent before each reply turn, pulling relevant context. Our memory system is thin — memory_search MCP tools exist but no proactive recall before agent turns.
features.implementation_notes: Add recall_phase in agent_core.py or prompt_builder.py that runs memory_search with user's latest message. Inject top-K results into system prompt. Make configurable (on/off, result count). Start with keyword search, evolve to semantic.

features.name: Move recalled memory to hidden untrusted prompt-prefix path
features.gap_analysis: Companion to #15. OpenClaw moved recalled memories from system prompt to untrusted prompt-prefix to prevent treating recalled content as instructions.
features.implementation_notes: When implementing active memory recall, inject recalled memories with [Recalled Context - untrusted] label in separate prompt section. Prevents prompt injection via poisoned memory.

features.name: QMD fix: stop treating lowercase memory.md as second root
features.gap_analysis: QMD-specific fix. Our memory system doesn't have dual-collection ambiguity.

features.name: Dreaming replay guard (require live cron event)
features.gap_analysis: Our heartbeat can trigger recurring actions. If scheduled sweep completes but subsequent heartbeat re-triggers it, we get duplicate processing.
features.implementation_notes: When implementing proactive memory maintenance, ensure heartbeat checks for active/consumed cron event before triggering sweep. Add last_sweep_time or event_consumed guard.

features.name: Telegram forum topic names in agent context
features.gap_analysis: Our Telegram bot handles messages but may not surface forum topic names as structured context for agent awareness.
features.implementation_notes: Extract forum topic name from incoming messages (forum_topic_created service messages, message_thread_id). Include in normalized message context.

features.name: Heartbeat topic isolation on Telegram forums
features.gap_analysis: Heartbeat replies in forum groups should stay in bound forum topic, not leak to group root chat.
features.implementation_notes: In heartbeat_adapter.py, preserve message_thread_id from triggering message. Verify reply goes to same forum topic.

features.name: Send policy fix (deny != block processing)
features.gap_analysis: We don't have an equivalent send-policy mechanism. Not applicable to current architecture.
features.implementation_notes: If we add listen-only mode later, decouple message processing from reply delivery.

features.name: Delivery queue persistence of session context
features.gap_analysis: If we queue outbound messages for delivery, we may lose session context on restart. OpenClaw now persists originating session context with queued entries.
features.implementation_notes: Audit outbound message delivery path. Ensure session context (routing target, delivery policy, origin) is persisted alongside message payload. Verify durable/checkpointing.py covers this.

features.name: Replace marked.js with markdown-it (ReDoS fix)
features.gap_analysis: If web-ui renders markdown using marked.js, vulnerable to ReDoS via maliciously crafted markdown.
features.implementation_notes: Check web-ui/package.json for markdown dependencies. If using marked, evaluate migration to markdown-it. At minimum add input sanitization/length limits.

features.name: Per-provider private network allowlist
features.gap_analysis: Self-hosted LLM endpoint config. We connect to external APIs directly.

features.name: Context engine ID validation
features.gap_analysis: If we load MCP tools dynamically, ID mismatch between registration and implementation could cause silent misbehavior.
features.implementation_notes: Add validation step in MCP tool initialization: verify tool's reported identity matches registration slot. Fail fast on mismatch.

features.name: Orphaned message recovery
features.gap_analysis: We handle messages sequentially. Orphaned messages less likely.
features.implementation_notes: Monitor for dropped messages in high-concurrency scenarios.

features.name: Turn maintenance as idle-aware background work
features.gap_analysis: We handle context management at prompt-builder level, sufficient for current scale.

features.name: OpenAI GPT reasoning effort mapping
features.gap_analysis: We don't use OpenAI reasoning effort parameter.

features.name: Narrow plugin loading to manifest-declared needs
features.gap_analysis: OpenClaw plugin system optimization. Our MCP tool loading is simpler.

features.name: Local loopback CDP bypass for SSRF policy
features.gap_analysis: Companion to SSRF enforcement (#2). Include loopback bypass when implementing #2.
features.implementation_notes: Include in SSRF implementation (#2) for own CDP control plane (localhost:9222).

features.name: macOS local MLX speech provider
features.gap_analysis: Platform-specific macOS feature.

features.name: Exec policy CLI commands
features.gap_analysis: We have guardrails but no CLI management interface for viewing/managing execution policies.
features.implementation_notes: Consider CLI for viewing/managing execution policies (allowed tools, sandbox rules). Low priority — our guardrails are primarily code-configured.

features.name: Hide synthetic transcript-repair tool results from chat
features.gap_analysis: Our web-ui may leak internal tool-call results into visible chat transcript.
features.implementation_notes: Filter out tool-call results from system/internal tools in web-ui display. Only show final text response and user-facing tool outputs.

features.name: Doctor: warn on orphaned agent directories
features.gap_analysis: Minor maintenance concern. Could add cleanup check for orphaned VP worker workspaces.
features.implementation_notes: Consider adding cleanup check in session reaper for orphaned VP workspaces.

top_5_priorities.name: Session routing metadata isolation
top_5_priorities.rationale: Prevents heartbeat/cron turns from poisoning shared session state. Architecturally critical.

top_5_priorities.name: SSRF policy on browser routes
top_5_priorities.rationale: Security hardening for agent-browser MCP tools. Recurring gap — escalating enforcement in OpenClaw.

top_5_priorities.name: Cron refire loop prevention + error-backoff preservation
top_5_priorities.rationale: Reliability fixes for our cron service. Prevents resource-wasting retry loops.

top_5_priorities.name: Hook:wake owner downgrade
top_5_priorities.rationale: Security: prevents privilege escalation via external webhook triggers.

top_5_priorities.name: Active Memory pattern
top_5_priorities.rationale: Largest effort but highest architectural impact for agent continuity across sessions.
